center.name: 东洋界
center.summary: The Indomalayan realm is one of the eight biogeographic realms.

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The pygmy bushtit is the only Indomalayan member of the Aegithalidae family. It is only found on the island of Java in Indonesia where it inhabits montane forests and plantations above 1,000 metres (3,300 ft), but can occasionally be seen as low as 830 metres (2,720 ft). They live on the edge of coniferous and open tree forests in the mountains of central and western Java

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: gulgula near Hyderabad, India | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Alauda | species = gulgula | authority = Franklin, 1831 | subdivision_ranks = Subspecies | subdivision = See text | synonyms = | range_map = Alauda gulgula distribution map.png }} The Oriental skylark (Alauda gulgula) is a species of skylark found in the Sino-Indian region and parts of central Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This raptor breeds primarily in the Palearctic and the Indomalayan regions. It also breeds in central and southeastern Europe; however, it is highly restricted to small, non-contiguous pockets in Poland, Belarus, Ukraine, Bulgaria, Romania, Serbia, and Hungary.Gabor, M
